ab: ka kakedrian aken imaza i kazangiljan a nia umaq, ka kitulu anga aken, setavat anga amen a mamaza i Akav, imaza amen sakamaya i lizuk a kikavaljuvaljut.
ch: 小時候我們家住在佳義，上學以後，就搬到了屏東，一直在都市裡生活。
en: When we were young, our family lived in Chiayi. After I started school, we moved to Pingtung and have been living in the city ever since.

ab: ka semupu anga aken tua sikipakelayan a kakituluan, kitulu aken tu masan italj tua pakata kakudakudan nua kacalisian a kakituluin, silautan tua namakakaizuazua namalavi a kakudakudan nua i taivang a kacalisian.
ch: 上了大學以後，我修了一堂關於原住民文化的課程，瞭解到臺灣原住民族有各式各樣豐富的文化。
en: After I went to college, I took a course on aboriginal culture and learned that the aboriginal people in Taiwan have various and rich cultures.

ab: vanasika, tucu a cavilj nu sikalja pulavayan tua kaljazulezulan, muri vayk amen kati kaka i Cemelesay a sema qinaljan a kivavadaq tua vavulungan, navenarung amen tu lemaut tua driniungan nua nia kakudakudan.
ch: 因此，今年暑假時，我和哥哥回到部落訪問老人家，想要瞭解自己族群的文化。
en: So this summer, my brother and I went back to our tribe to visit the elderly, hoping to understand the culture of our own ethnic group.

ab: azua vavulungan alu anga puluq a cavilj, ljakua nu tjapacunan matu i cengeceng a kinacaviljan.
ch: 老人家已經八十歲了，但是看起來還像中年人。
en: The old man is already eighty years old, but he still looks like a middle-aged man.

ab: ku kivadaqan timadju tu anemanema nu kayan a kacaquin, aza nimadju a Vinuculjan(Pinaiwanan) sulapiq a siqaqivuan, macaqu uta qivu tua Drinkayan, Kinilangan kata Drinipungan.
ch: 我問他會哪些語言，他的排灣語說得很流利，也會說魯凱族語、客家話和日本話。
en: I asked him which languages he knew, and he was fluent in Paiwan, as well as Rukai, Hakka and Japanese.

ab: ka ku kivadaqan anga timadju kemljang anga aken, tu manu kemasi kedrikedri a kicaquan tua [Drinipungan, a cekelj seRukai, au aza itua sengesengan nasekeljang tua tjuruvu aravac a Kilang, vanasika liav a kacaquin a kay nimadju.
ch: 我問他才知道，原來小時候就學日本話，太太是魯凱族人，而在工作上認識許多客家人，所以他會說這麼多語言。
en: I asked him to find out that he learned Japanese when he was a child, his wife is an Rukai, and he knows many Hakkas at work, so he can speak so many languages.

ab: nu kivadavadaq amen, aza ramaljemaljeng masan lialiav aravac a pakata kinasicuay a kinljangan tua siqaljupan a sipareqaivuivu.
ch: 我們訪談的時候，老人家談論了許多關於狩獵的傳統知識。
en: During our interview, the old man talked about a lot of traditional knowledge about hunting.

ab: uitu inika amen a makapucug a pipenuljatan, ljakua nia sinipuseqetj anga paseqailjungan a pitua pinilima a ljingas, makaya siumaljanan sa sileqeleq a kisalangda.
ch: 雖然我們沒有辦法完全聽懂，但是我們有用手機錄影，可以再仔細聆聽。
en: Although we could’t fully understand it, we could record it on our cell phones and listen to it more.

ab: ka kakedrianan aken ika aken a nalemaut tua kakudakudan nua ku kinadriungan, pitua ku kinakacalisianan izua za ku siaqan.
ch: 小時候我不瞭解自己族群的文化，對於自己是原住民有點不好意思。
en: When I was young, I didn’t understand the culture of my ethnic group, and I was a little embarrassed that I was an aborigine.

ab: nu tjaliav anga ku linaut, patjazua ku kinavarungan tua kinasePaiwanan, na remamur a kinisumaljian, mapuqata uta a simiazuan.
ch: 瞭解愈多以後，愈覺得自己身為排灣族人，是很獨特的，也很值得驕傲。
en: The more I know, the more I feel that being a Paiwan is unique and something to be proud of.
